# Irrlicht — Development Guide

## Project Structure

- `core/` — Go daemon and CLI tools (module: `irrlicht/core`)
- `platforms/macos/` — Swift macOS menu bar app (SwiftPM)
- `site/` — Landing page and documentation (static HTML, GitHub Pages)

## Build Artifacts

**Do not create `bin/` or build outputs in the project tree.** Use `/tmp/` for build artifacts.

```bash
# Go daemon
cd core && go build -o /tmp/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd

# CLI tool
cd core && go build -o /tmp/irrlicht-ls ./cmd/irrlicht-ls

# Swift app (uses .build/ which is gitignored)
cd platforms/macos && swift build
```

The only legitimate build directory is `platforms/macos/.build/` (managed by SwiftPM, gitignored).

## Testing

```bash
cd core && go test ./... -count=1
```

## Release Process

Use `/ir:release` (patch), `/ir:release minor`, or `/ir:release major`. See `.claude/skills/ir:release/skill.md` for the full pipeline.

## Key Conventions

- Go code follows hexagonal architecture: `domain/` → `ports/` → `adapters/` → `application/services/`
- SessionDetector delegates to: `StateClassifier`, `MetadataEnricher`, `PIDManager`
- Three session states only: `working`, `waiting`, `ready` — no cancelled state
- Errors are logged via `Logger` interface, not propagated with `fmt.Errorf`
- Child sessions (subagents) use `ParentSessionID` for parent-child linking

.claude/skills/ir:test-mac/SKILL.md

---
name: ir:test-mac
description: >
  Build and restart the irrlicht daemon and macOS Swift app for local testing.
  Compiles the Go daemon, builds the Swift app, kills all running instances
  (including production Irrlicht.app), and launches the freshly built versions.
  Use when the user says "test mac", "restart mac", "rebuild mac", or "/ir:test-mac".
---

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.
- Daemon logs: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`
- Swift app logs: `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`

Done — I followed the `ir:test-mac` flow and rebuilt/restarted the local mac dev stack.

### What I ran
- Built daemon: `core/cmd/irrlichd` → **`/tmp/irrlichd`**  
  (used `/tmp` per project build-artifact rule)
- Built Swift app in `platforms/macos` (`swift build`)
- Killed existing Irrlicht app/daemon processes
- Removed stale socket: `/Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock`
- Started dev daemon with logs at: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`
- Confirmed daemon listening on `:7837`
- Started dev app with logs at: `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`
- Verified both processes + API response

### Verification
- Daemon PID: `17808`
- App PID: `18073`
- `curl http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions` returned session JSON successfully.

If you want, I can also tail both log files for 30s and report any startup warnings.

{"type":"message","id":"b2077873","parentId":"d589a1e1","timestamp":"2026-04-06T16:40:32.389Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_EeoGJ2eWUtSqneFtJlQXx73L|fc_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3e2000fd88191b7e0278461955044","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"package pi

import (
	"irrlicht/core/pkg/tailer"
)

// Parser implements tailer.TranscriptParser for Pi coding agent transcripts.
// Pi nests role, stopReason, content, and usage inside a "message" object:
//
//	{"type": "message", "message": {"role": "assistant", "stopReason": "stop", ...}}
type Parser struct{}

// ParseLine parses a Pi JSONL line into a normalized ParsedEvent.
func (p *Parser) ParseLine(raw map[string]interface{}) *tailer.ParsedEvent {
	ev := &tailer.ParsedEvent{
		Timestamp: tailer.ParseTimestamp(raw),
	}

	eventType := ""
	if et, ok := raw["type"].(string); ok {
		eventType = et
	}

	// No type field → skip (shouldn't happen for Pi but be defensive).
	if eventType == "" {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Session header: {"type": "session", "version": 3, "cwd": "..."}
	if eventType == "session" {
		if cwd, ok := raw["cwd"].(string); ok && cwd != "" {
			ev.CWD = cwd
		}
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Model change — extract model info and skip.
	if eventType == "model_change" {
		if model, ok := raw["modelId"].(string); ok && model != "" {
			ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(model)
		}
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Non-message metadata types — skip.
	switch eventType {
	case "thinking_level_change", "compaction", "branch_summary", "custom":
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// All remaining Pi events should be type: "message" with a nested message object.
	if eventType != "message" {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	piMsg, ok := raw["message"].(map[string]interface{})
	if !ok {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	role, _ := piMsg["role"].(string)

	switch role {
	case "assistant":
		stopReason, _ := piMsg["stopReason"].(string)
		if stopReason == "stop" {
			ev.EventType = "assistant_message" // end-of-turn
		} else {
			ev.EventType = "assistant" // mid-turn (toolUse, etc.)
		}

		// Extract tool calls from message.content[].
		if contentArr, ok := piMsg["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
			for _, item := range contentArr {
				if block, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
					if block["type"] == "toolCall" {
						if name, ok := block["name"].(string); ok {
							ev.ToolUseNames = append(ev.ToolUseNames, name)
						}
					}
				}
			}
		}

		// Extract assistant text for waiting-state display.
		ev.AssistantText = extractPiAssistantText(piMsg)

		// Model and tokens from the message.
		if model, ok := piMsg["model"].(string); ok && model != "" {
			ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(model)
		}
		if usage, ok := piMsg["usage"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
			ev.Tokens = tailer.ExtractUsage(usage)
		}

	case "user":
		ev.EventType = "user_message"
		ev.ClearToolNames = true

	case "toolResult":
		ev.EventType = "tool_result"
		ev.ToolResultCount = 1 // Single canonical count — NOT also counted in addMessageEvent.
		if isErr, ok := piMsg["isError"].(bool); ok && isErr {
			ev.IsError = true
		}

	case "bashExecution":
		// User-side shell escape (! command) — skip.
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev

	default:
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Content character count.
	ev.ContentChars = tailer.ExtractContentChars(raw)

	return ev
}

// extractPiAssistantText extracts text from Pi's nested message.content[] blocks.
func extractPiAssistantText(piMsg map[string]interface{}) string {
	contentArr, ok := piMsg["content"].([]interface{})
	if !ok {
		return ""
	}
	var text string
	for _, item := range contentArr {
		if block, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
			if block["type"] == "text" {
				if t, ok := block["text"].(string); ok && t != "" {
					text = t // Use the last text block.
				}
			}
		}
	}
	if len([]rune(text)) > 200 {
		return string([]rune(text)[:200])
	}
	return text

{"type":"message","id":"b2077873","parentId":"d589a1e1","timestamp":"2026-04-06T16:40:32.389Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_EeoGJ2eWUtSqneFtJlQXx73L|fc_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3e2000fd88191b7e0278461955044","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"package pi

import (
	"irrlicht/core/pkg/tailer"
)

// Parser implements tailer.TranscriptParser for Pi coding agent transcripts.
// Pi nests role, stopReason, content, and usage inside a "message" object:
//
//	{"type": "message", "message": {"role": "assistant", "stopReason": "stop", ...}}
type Parser struct{}

// ParseLine parses a Pi JSONL line into a normalized ParsedEvent.
func (p *Parser) ParseLine(raw map[string]interface{}) *tailer.ParsedEvent {
	ev := &tailer.ParsedEvent{
		Timestamp: tailer.ParseTimestamp(raw),
	}

	eventType := ""
	if et, ok := raw["type"].(string); ok {
		eventType = et
	}

	// No type field → skip (shouldn't happen for Pi but be defensive).
	if eventType == "" {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Session header: {"type": "session", "version": 3, "cwd": "..."}
	if eventType == "session" {
		if cwd, ok := raw["cwd"].(string); ok && cwd != "" {
			ev.CWD = cwd
		}
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Model change — extract model info and skip.
	if eventType == "model_change" {
		if model, ok := raw["modelId"].(string); ok && model != "" {
			ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(model)
		}
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Non-message metadata types — skip.
	switch eventType {
	case "thinking_level_change", "compaction", "branch_summary", "custom":
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// All remaining Pi events should be type: "message" with a nested message object.
	if eventType != "message" {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	piMsg, ok := raw["message"].(map[string]interface{})
	if !ok {
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	role, _ := piMsg["role"].(string)

	switch role {
	case "assistant":
		stopReason, _ := piMsg["stopReason"].(string)
		if stopReason == "stop" {
			ev.EventType = "assistant_message" // end-of-turn
		} else {
			ev.EventType = "assistant" // mid-turn (toolUse, etc.)
		}

		// Extract tool calls from message.content[].
		if contentArr, ok := piMsg["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
			for _, item := range contentArr {
				if block, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
					if block["type"] == "toolCall" {
						if name, ok := block["name"].(string); ok {
							ev.ToolUseNames = append(ev.ToolUseNames, name)
						}
					}
				}
			}
		}

		// Extract assistant text for waiting-state display.
		ev.AssistantText = extractPiAssistantText(piMsg)

		// Model and tokens from the message.
		if model, ok := piMsg["model"].(string); ok && model != "" {
			ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(model)
		}
		if usage, ok := piMsg["usage"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
			ev.Tokens = tailer.ExtractUsage(usage)
		}

	case "user":
		ev.EventType = "user_message"
		ev.ClearToolNames = true

	case "toolResult":
		ev.EventType = "tool_result"
		ev.ToolResultCount = 1 // Single canonical count — NOT also counted in addMessageEvent.
		if isErr, ok := piMsg["isError"].(bool); ok && isErr {
			ev.IsError = true
		}

	case "bashExecution":
		// User-side shell escape (! command) — skip.
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev

	default:
		ev.Skip = true
		return ev
	}

	// Content character count.
	ev.ContentChars = tailer.ExtractContentChars(raw)

	return ev
}

// extractPiAssistantText extracts text from Pi's nested message.content[] blocks.
func extractPiAssistantText(piMsg map[string]interface{}) string {
	contentArr, ok := piMsg["content"].([]interface{})
	if !ok {
		return ""
	}
	var text string
	for _, item := range contentArr {
		if block, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
			if block["type"] == "text" {
				if t, ok := block["text"].(string); ok && t != "" {
					text = t // Use the last text block.
				}
			}
		}
	}
	if len([]rune(text)) > 200 {
		return string([]rune(text)[:200])
	}
	return text
}
"}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1775493632389}}

Found it. Root cause is in the **Pi parser/state pipeline**, not your session file.

### What happened in your session
In transcript  
`/Users/ingo/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl`

there is a compaction event:

- line 54: `{"type":"compaction", ... "timestamp":"2026-04-06T16:39:06.518Z", ...}`

### Why it stayed `ready`
In `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`, compaction is explicitly ignored:

- `case "thinking_level_change", "compaction", "branch_summary", "custom":`
- `ev.Skip = true`

So compaction produces **no ParsedEvent message event**, no `LastEventType` update, no tool-open signal, no “working” hint.

Then classifier logic only promotes to working on transcript activity that yields actionable metrics, and with no compaction signal it remains/returns `ready`.

### Additional design gap
You already have `CompactionState` in domain model (`session.go`), but it is not being set anywhere for Pi (or generally in the current path). So there is no dedicated compaction-state transition in runtime state.

---

## Cause summary
**Pi compaction events are dropped by parser, so SessionDetector never sees compaction as active work.**  
Result: session appears `ready` while compact is running.

If you want, I can patch this so `type:"compaction"` maps to a working signal (and optionally set `compaction_state=compacting/post_compact`) with tests.
ok, create a new branch, create tests, work on the fix. check that fix works and commit pr.

**File:** `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`

- It is now parsed as an activity event (`EventType = "assistant"`), so it contributes to ready→working detection.

### Tests added
**File:** `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser_test.go`
- `TestParser_Compaction_IsActivityEvent`
- `TestParser_Compaction_SetsLastEventToAssistant`

arguments.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from collections import Counter
p='/Users/ingo/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl'
uses=[]; results=[]; q_assist=0;assist_stop=0
with open(p) as f:
    for ln in f:
        ln=ln.strip()
        if not ln: continue
        try:d=json.loads(ln)
        except: continue
        if d.get('type')!='message': continue
        m=d.get('message',{})
        r=m.get('role')
        if r=='assistant':
            if m.get('stopReason')=='stop': assist_stop+=1
            txt=''
            for b in m.get('content',[]) or []:
                if isinstance(b,dict) and b.get('type')=='text': txt=b.get('text','')
                if isinstance(b,dict) and b.get('type')=='toolCall':
                    uses.append((b.get('id'),b.get('name')))
            if txt.endswith('?'): q_assist+=1
        elif r=='toolResult':
            results.append(m.get('toolCallId'))

uc=Counter([u[0] for u in uses if u[0]])
rc=Counter([r for r in results if r])
open_ids=[(i,uc[i]-rc[i], next((n for x,n in uses if x==i),None)) for i in uc if uc[i]-rc[i]>0]
print('uses',len(uses),'results',len(results),'open_ids',len(open_ids),'assist_stop',assist_stop,'assist_questions',q_assist)
print('open sample',open_ids[-10:])
# Also detect result without use
orph=[(i,rc[i]-uc[i]) for i in rc if rc[i]-uc[i]>0]
print('orphan results',len(orph), 'sample', orph[:5])
PY

from collections import Counter
p='/Users/ingo/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl'
uses=[]; results=[]; q_assist=0;assist_stop=0

    for ln in f:
        ln=ln.strip()

        try:d=json.loads(ln)
        except: continue
        if d.get('type')!='message': continue
        m=d.get('message',{})

            if m.get('stopReason')=='stop': assist_stop+=1

        elif r=='toolResult':
            results.append(m.get('toolCallId'))

rc=Counter([r for r in results if r])
open_ids=[(i,uc[i]-rc[i], next((n for x,n in uses if x==i),None)) for i in uc if uc[i]-rc[i]>0]

# Also detect result without use
orph=[(i,rc[i]-uc[i]) for i in rc if rc[i]-uc[i]>0]
print('orphan results',len(orph), 'sample', orph[:5])

I checked that exact Pi session transcript and the detector logic.  
The main issue is **false waiting from stale-tool timeout**.

## What’s going wrong

1. **Detector auto-transitions to `waiting` after 5s of no activity** when a non-user-blocking tool call is open:
   - `core/application/services/session_detector.go`
   - `defaultStaleToolTimeout = 5 * time.Second`
   - transition reason: `"open tool call with no activity → waiting (likely permission-pending)"`

2. In this session, there are multiple **normal bash tool calls lasting >5s**:
   - ~12.7s
   - ~10.7s
   - ~13.4s  
   During these periods, Pi is still legitimately processing, but detector assumes “permission-pending” and marks `waiting`.

3. For Pi, this is amplified because **permission mode is not being provided/parsing doesn’t set `PermissionMode`**, so the guard:
   - `state.Metrics.PermissionMode != "bypassPermissions"`
   is effectively always true, meaning stale-tool timer is always eligible.

## Why it can look wrong “after finished”

If the session got pushed into `waiting` by stale timeout, it only corrects on subsequent activity/classification. Depending on event sequence, it can appear stuck in `waiting` longer than expected even though from user perspective work is done.

---

So root cause is not random state flapping: it’s the **stale-tool heuristic tuned too aggressively for Pi’s normal tool durations + missing permission-mode signal for Pi**.  

If you want, I can implement the fix next (adapter-aware timeout/disable for Pi, or safer stale-tool conditions).

i think we should pull this logic out to the adapter. the adapter should handle the logic.

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_SkipsBypassPermissions(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "bypassPermissions",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
	}

	// Wait past timeout.
	time.Sleep(250 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("bypass1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want working (bypassPermissions should not trigger stale timer)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_GuardChecksState(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Read"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "default",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
	}

	// Manually change session state to "ready" before timer fires.
	time.Sleep(30 * time.Millisecond)
	state, _ := repo.Load("guard1")
	state.State = session.StateReady
	state.UpdatedAt = time.Now().Unix() // different from expectedUpdatedAt
	repo.Save(state)

	// Wait for timer to fire.
	time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ = repo.Load("guard1")
	if state.State != session.StateReady {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want ready (guard should prevent overwrite)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_ParentHeldWorking_WhenChildrenActive(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetector(tw, pw, repo)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()

	// Parent session: turn is done, no open tool calls.
	// Without children this would transition to ready.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "turn_done",
			HasOpenToolCall:   false,
			LastAssistantText: "Done.",
		},
	})

	// Child session: still working.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:       "child1",
		State:           session.StateWorking,
		ParentSessionID: "parent1",
		TranscriptPath:  "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1/subagents/child1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:       now,
		UpdatedAt:       now,
		EventCount:      3,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
		},
	})

	// Trigger parent activity — should be held in working because child is active.
	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
	}

	time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("parent1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("parent state: got %q, want working (child still active)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_SkipsBypassPermissions(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "bypassPermissions",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
	}

	// Wait past timeout.
	time.Sleep(250 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("bypass1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want working (bypassPermissions should not trigger stale timer)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_GuardChecksState(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Read"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "default",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
	}

	// Manually change session state to "ready" before timer fires.
	time.Sleep(30 * time.Millisecond)
	state, _ := repo.Load("guard1")
	state.State = session.StateReady
	state.UpdatedAt = time.Now().Unix() // different from expectedUpdatedAt
	repo.Save(state)

	// Wait for timer to fire.
	time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ = repo.Load("guard1")
	if state.State != session.StateReady {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want ready (guard should prevent overwrite)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_ParentHeldWorking_WhenChildrenActive(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetector(tw, pw, repo)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()

	// Parent session: turn is done, no open tool calls.
	// Without children this would transition to ready.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "turn_done",
			HasOpenToolCall:   false,
			LastAssistantText: "Done.",
		},
	})

	// Child session: still working.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:       "child1",
		State:           session.StateWorking,
		ParentSessionID: "parent1",
		TranscriptPath:  "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1/subagents/child1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:       now,
		UpdatedAt:       now,
		EventCount:      3,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
		},
	})

	// Trigger parent activity — should be held in working because child is active.
	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
	}

	time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("parent1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("parent state: got %q, want working (child still active)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

The main issue is **false waiting from stale-tool timeout**.

## What’s going wrong

1. **Detector auto-transitions to `waiting` after 5s of no activity** when a non-user-blocking tool call is open:
   - `core/application/services/session_detector.go`
   - `defaultStaleToolTimeout = 5 * time.Second`
   - transition reason: `"open tool call with no activity → waiting (likely permission-pending)"`

2. In this session, there are multiple **normal bash tool calls lasting >5s**:
   - ~12.7s
   - ~10.7s
   - ~13.4s  
   During these periods, Pi is still legitimately processing, but detector assumes “permission-pending” and marks `waiting`.

3. For Pi, this is amplified because **permission mode is not being provided/parsing doesn’t set `PermissionMode`**, so the guard:
   - `state.Metrics.PermissionMode != "bypassPermissions"`
   is effectively always true, meaning stale-tool timer is always eligible.

## Why it can look wrong “after finished”

If the session got pushed into `waiting` by stale timeout, it only corrects on subsequent activity/classification. Depending on event sequence, it can appear stuck in `waiting` longer than expected even though from user perspective work is done.

---

So root cause is not random state flapping: it’s the **stale-tool heuristic tuned too aggressively for Pi’s normal tool durations + missing permission-mode signal for Pi**.  

If you want, I can implement the fix next (adapter-aware timeout/disable for Pi, or safer stale-tool conditions).","textSignature":"{"v":1,"id":"msg_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3e488c31c8191878efe3ed95ae1ab","phase":"final_answer"}"}],"api":"openai-codex-responses","provider":"openai-codex","model":"gpt-5.3-codex","usage":{"input":14734,"output":442,"cacheRead":80512,"cacheWrite":0,"totalTokens":95688,"cost":{"input":0.0257845,"output":0.006188,"cacheRead":0.014089599999999999,"cacheWrite":0,"total":0.0460621}},"stopReason":"stop","timestamp":1775494276410,"responseId":"resp_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3e485d1248191a3d4703783f2093d"}}

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_SkipsBypassPermissions(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "bypassPermissions",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "bypass1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/bypass1.jsonl",
	}

	// Wait past timeout.
	time.Sleep(250 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("bypass1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want working (bypassPermissions should not trigger stale timer)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_StaleToolCall_GuardChecksState(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetectorWithStaleTimeout(tw, pw, repo, 100*time.Millisecond)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			OpenToolCallCount: 1,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Read"},
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			PermissionMode:    "default",
		},
	})

	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "guard1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/guard1.jsonl",
	}

	// Manually change session state to "ready" before timer fires.
	time.Sleep(30 * time.Millisecond)
	state, _ := repo.Load("guard1")
	state.State = session.StateReady
	state.UpdatedAt = time.Now().Unix() // different from expectedUpdatedAt
	repo.Save(state)

	// Wait for timer to fire.
	time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ = repo.Load("guard1")
	if state.State != session.StateReady {
		t.Errorf("state: got %q, want ready (guard should prevent overwrite)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

func TestSessionDetector_ParentHeldWorking_WhenChildrenActive(t *testing.T) {
	tw := newMockAgentWatcher()
	pw := newMockProcessWatcher()
	repo := newMockRepo()

	det := newDetector(tw, pw, repo)

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() { done <- det.Run(ctx) }()

	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)

	now := time.Now().Unix()

	// Parent session: turn is done, no open tool calls.
	// Without children this would transition to ready.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		State:          session.StateWorking,
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:      now,
		UpdatedAt:      now,
		EventCount:     5,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "turn_done",
			HasOpenToolCall:   false,
			LastAssistantText: "Done.",
		},
	})

	// Child session: still working.
	repo.Save(&session.SessionState{
		SessionID:       "child1",
		State:           session.StateWorking,
		ParentSessionID: "parent1",
		TranscriptPath:  "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1/subagents/child1.jsonl",
		FirstSeen:       now,
		UpdatedAt:       now,
		EventCount:      3,
		Metrics: &session.SessionMetrics{
			LastEventType:     "assistant",
			HasOpenToolCall:   true,
			LastOpenToolNames: []string{"Bash"},
		},
	})

	// Trigger parent activity — should be held in working because child is active.
	tw.ch <- agent.Event{
		Type:           agent.EventActivity,
		SessionID:      "parent1",
		ProjectDir:     "-Users-test",
		TranscriptPath: "/home/.claude/projects/-Users-test/parent1.jsonl",
	}

	time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)

	state, _ := repo.Load("parent1")
	if state.State != session.StateWorking {
		t.Errorf("parent state: got %q, want working (child still active)", state.State)
	}

	cancel()
	<-done
}

Yes — your instinct is right.

## Verdict
**Adapter-specific waiting logic is the right direction**, especially for stale-tool timeout behavior.

Right now, stale timeout is global in `SessionDetector` and assumes permission gating semantics that fit Claude/Codex better than Pi. That’s why Pi gets false `waiting`.

## Recommended approach (clean + incremental)

### 1) Introduce adapter state policy (small interface)
Create something like:

- `ShouldUseStaleToolTimer(metrics, state) bool`
- `StaleToolTimeout() time.Duration`
- (optional) `IsUserBlockingTool(name string) bool`

### 2) Keep core state machine shared
Don’t move *all* classification to adapters. Keep shared `working/ready` logic in core, but delegate **adapter semantics** (especially waiting heuristics).

### 3) Implement per-adapter policies
- **pi**: disable stale-tool timer (or set much higher timeout) until Pi emits explicit permission-pending signal.
- **claude-code/codex**: keep current behavior.

### 4) Wire SessionDetector to policy by `state.Adapter`
Replace hardcoded stale-tool checks with policy calls.

---

## Why this is better than full adapter-owned state
If you move complete state transitions into each adapter, you’ll duplicate logic and drift behavior.  
A policy hook gives you adapter control where needed, while keeping one lifecycle engine.

---

## For your current bug
This would directly prevent Pi from incorrectly entering `waiting` during normal long-running `bash/read/write` tool calls.

If you want, I can implement this as a focused PR (policy interface + Pi disables stale timer + regression tests).

## Summary
- treat Pi `type: "compaction"` transcript entries as activity events instead of skipping them
- map compaction to `assistant` activity so ready sessions transition to `working`
- pull stale-tool waiting behavior behind adapter policies
  - add `agent.StatePolicy`
  - add `StatePolicy()` in Claude Code, Codex, and Pi adapters
  - wire policies into `SessionDetector` via `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
  - disable stale-tool timeout waiting for Pi to avoid false `waiting` while long-running tools are still processing
- add tests for Pi compaction parsing, adapter policies, and stale-tool policy disabling

## Verification
- `cd core && go test ./application/services ./adapters/inbound/agents/pi ./adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode ./adapters/inbound/agents/codex -count=1`
- `cd core && go test ./... -count=1`
EOF

content.text: https://github.com/ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht/pull/91
## fix/pi-compaction-working-state...origin/fix/pi-compaction-working-state

Implemented ✅ — adapter-specific waiting logic is now in place.

### What I changed

On branch **`fix/pi-compaction-working-state`**:

1. **Added adapter state policy model**
   - `core/domain/agent/state_policy.go`
   - New `StatePolicy` with:
     - `EnableStaleToolTimer`
     - `StaleToolTimeout` (override support)

2. **Moved stale-tool behavior ownership to adapters**
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy.go` → stale timer **disabled**

3. **SessionDetector now consumes adapter policies**
   - `core/application/services/session_detector.go`
   - Added:
     - `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
     - adapter policy resolution helpers
     - stale timeout per adapter
     - `shouldStartStaleToolTimer(...)`
   - Replaced hardcoded stale-tool conditions with policy-driven checks in both:
     - live activity path
     - startup seed path
   - stale timeout callback now also re-validates policy

4. **Wired policies at startup**
   - `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go`
   - `detector.SetAdapterPolicies(...)` using each adapter’s `StatePolicy()`

---

### Tests added/updated

- `core/application/services/session_detector_test.go`
  - Added regression: **Pi policy disables stale-tool waiting**
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy_test.go`

All tests pass:
- `cd core && go test ./... -count=1` ✅

---

### Commit & PR

- **Commit:** `6b4fed1`
- **Pushed:** `fix/pi-compaction-working-state`
- **PR updated:** https://github.com/ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht/pull/91

---
name: ir:test-mac
description: >
  Build and restart the irrlicht daemon and macOS Swift app for local testing.
  Compiles the Go daemon, builds the Swift app, kills all running instances
  (including production Irrlicht.app), and launches the freshly built versions.
  Use when the user says "test mac", "restart mac", "rebuild mac", or "/ir:test-mac".
---

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.
- Daemon logs: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`
- Swift app logs: `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`

## Summary
- treat Pi `type: "compaction"` transcript entries as activity events instead of skipping them
- map compaction to `assistant` activity so ready sessions transition to `working`
- pull stale-tool waiting behavior behind adapter policies
  - add `agent.StatePolicy`
  - add `StatePolicy()` in Claude Code, Codex, and Pi adapters
  - wire policies into `SessionDetector` via `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
  - disable stale-tool timeout waiting for Pi to avoid false `waiting` while long-running tools are still processing
- add tests for Pi compaction parsing, adapter policies, and stale-tool policy disabling

## Verification
- `cd core && go test ./application/services ./adapters/inbound/agents/pi ./adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode ./adapters/inbound/agents/codex -count=1`

### What I changed

On branch **`fix/pi-compaction-working-state`**:

1. **Added adapter state policy model**
   - `core/domain/agent/state_policy.go`
   - New `StatePolicy` with:
     - `EnableStaleToolTimer`
     - `StaleToolTimeout` (override support)

2. **Moved stale-tool behavior ownership to adapters**
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy.go` → stale timer **disabled**

3. **SessionDetector now consumes adapter policies**
   - `core/application/services/session_detector.go`
   - Added:
     - `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
     - adapter policy resolution helpers
     - stale timeout per adapter
     - `shouldStartStaleToolTimer(...)`
   - Replaced hardcoded stale-tool conditions with policy-driven checks in both:
     - live activity path
     - startup seed path
   - stale timeout callback now also re-validates policy

4. **Wired policies at startup**
   - `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go`
   - `detector.SetAdapterPolicies(...)` using each adapter’s `StatePolicy()`

---

### Tests added/updated

- `core/application/services/session_detector_test.go`

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy_test.go`

---
name: ir:test-mac
description: >
  Build and restart the irrlicht daemon and macOS Swift app for local testing.
  Compiles the Go daemon, builds the Swift app, kills all running instances
  (including production Irrlicht.app), and launches the freshly built versions.
  Use when the user says "test mac", "restart mac", "rebuild mac", or "/ir:test-mac".
---

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.

## Summary
- treat Pi `type: "compaction"` transcript entries as activity events instead of skipping them
- map compaction to `assistant` activity so ready sessions transition to `working`
- pull stale-tool waiting behavior behind adapter policies
  - add `agent.StatePolicy`
  - add `StatePolicy()` in Claude Code, Codex, and Pi adapters
  - wire policies into `SessionDetector` via `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
  - disable stale-tool timeout waiting for Pi to avoid false `waiting` while long-running tools are still processing
- add tests for Pi compaction parsing, adapter policies, and stale-tool policy disabling

## Verification
- `cd core && go test ./application/services ./adapters/inbound/agents/pi ./adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode ./adapters/inbound/agents/codex -count=1`

### What I changed

On branch **`fix/pi-compaction-working-state`**:

1. **Added adapter state policy model**
   - `core/domain/agent/state_policy.go`
   - New `StatePolicy` with:
     - `EnableStaleToolTimer`
     - `StaleToolTimeout` (override support)

2. **Moved stale-tool behavior ownership to adapters**
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy.go` → stale timer enabled
   - `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy.go` → stale timer **disabled**

3. **SessionDetector now consumes adapter policies**
   - `core/application/services/session_detector.go`
   - Added:
     - `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
     - adapter policy resolution helpers
     - stale timeout per adapter
     - `shouldStartStaleToolTimer(...)`
   - Replaced hardcoded stale-tool conditions with policy-driven checks in both:
     - live activity path
     - startup seed path
   - stale timeout callback now also re-validates policy

4. **Wired policies at startup**
   - `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go`
   - `detector.SetAdapterPolicies(...)` using each adapter’s `StatePolicy()`

---

### Tests added/updated

- `core/application/services/session_detector_test.go`

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/policy_test.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/policy_test.go`

---
name: ir:test-mac
description: >
  Build and restart the irrlicht daemon and macOS Swift app for local testing.
  Compiles the Go daemon, builds the Swift app, kills all running instances
  (including production Irrlicht.app), and launches the freshly built versions.
  Use when the user says "test mac", "restart mac", "rebuild mac", or "/ir:test-mac".
---

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.

// --- Agent subagent tool name tracking tests (issue #88) ---

func TestLastOpenToolNames_AgentToolsPreservedAfterPartialResults(t *testing.T) {
	// Simulate Claude Code format: 3 streaming assistant events each with one
	// Agent tool_use, followed by 1 user event carrying a tool_result.
	// After the first result, 2 Agent calls remain open — LastOpenToolNames
	// must still contain them so InferSubagents can count them.
	path := writeTranscriptLines(t, []map[string]interface{}{
		// 3 streaming assistant chunks, each with one Agent tool_use
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(0), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": nil,
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(1), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": nil,
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(2), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": "tool_use",
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		// First tool_result arrives (user event with embedded tool_result)
		{"type": "user", "timestamp": ts(3), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "user",
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_result", "content": "done"},
			},
		}},
	})

	tailer := newTestTailer(path)
	m, err := tailer.TailAndProcess()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	// 3 uses - 1 result = 2 open
	if !m.HasOpenToolCall {
		t.Error("expected HasOpenToolCall=true with 2 unmatched Agent calls")
	}
	if m.OpenToolCallCount != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected OpenToolCallCount=2, got %d", m.OpenToolCallCount)
	}

	// BUG (issue #88): ClearToolNames on the user event wipes LastOpenToolNames
	// even though tool_result blocks are present. The remaining 2 Agent names
	// should be preserved so InferSubagents can detect them.
	if len(m.LastOpenToolNames) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected LastOpenToolNames to have 2 entries, got %d: %v",
			len(m.LastOpenToolNames), m.LastOpenToolNames)
	}
	for i, name := range m.LastOpenToolNames {
		if name != "Agent" {
			t.Errorf("LastOpenToolNames[%d] = %q, want \"Agent\"", i, name)
		}
	}
}

{"type":"message","id":"2b630846","parentId":"337be807","timestamp":"2026-04-06T17:22:30.620Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_swlVJoAcXApmTwY5TmCVNjns|fc_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3ebd5ff8881919f0b1118373fefaf","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"package filesystem

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"irrlicht/core/domain/session"
)

const appSupportDir = "Library/Application Support/Irrlicht"

// SessionRepository implements ports/outbound.SessionRepository using the local filesystem.
type SessionRepository struct {
	instancesDir string
}

// New returns a SessionRepository rooted at the user's Application Support directory.
func New() (*SessionRepository, error) {
	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
	}
	return &SessionRepository{
		instancesDir: filepath.Join(homeDir, appSupportDir, "instances"),
	}, nil
}

// NewWithDir returns a SessionRepository rooted at the given directory (useful for tests).
func NewWithDir(dir string) *SessionRepository {
	return &SessionRepository{instancesDir: dir}
}

// InstancesDir returns the directory where session files are stored.
func (r *SessionRepository) InstancesDir() string {
	return r.instancesDir
}

// Load reads a session state from disk. Returns (nil, err) if the file does not exist.
func (r *SessionRepository) Load(sessionID string) (*session.SessionState, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(r.statePath(sessionID))
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	var state session.SessionState
	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &state, nil
}

// Save atomically writes a session state to disk.
func (r *SessionRepository) Save(state *session.SessionState) error {
	if err := os.MkdirAll(r.instancesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create instances directory: %w", err)
	}
	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(state, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal session state: %w", err)
	}
	path := r.statePath(state.SessionID)
	tmpPath := fmt.Sprintf("%s.tmp.%d.%d", path, os.Getpid(), time.Now().UnixNano())
	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp file: %w", err)
	}
	if err := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); err != nil {
		os.Remove(tmpPath)
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename temp file: %w", err)
	}
	return nil
}

// Delete removes a session state file. Returns nil if the file does not exist.
func (r *SessionRepository) Delete(sessionID string) error {
	err := os.Remove(r.statePath(sessionID))
	if err != nil && os.IsNotExist(err) {
		return nil
	}
	return err
}

// ListAll returns all session states found in the instances directory.
// Files that cannot be parsed are silently skipped.
func (r *SessionRepository) ListAll() ([]*session.SessionState, error) {
	entries, err := os.ReadDir(r.instancesDir)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return nil, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}
	var states []*session.SessionState
	for _, entry := range entries {
		if entry.IsDir() {
			continue
		}
		name := entry.Name()
		if filepath.Ext(name) != ".json" || strings.Contains(name, ".tmp.") {
			continue
		}
		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, name))
		if err != nil {
			continue
		}
		var state session.SessionState
		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
			continue
		}
		validStates := map[string]bool{
			session.StateWorking: true,
			session.StateWaiting: true,
			session.StateReady:   true,
		}
		if !validStates[state.State] {
			os.Remove(filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, name))
			continue
		}
		states = append(states, &state)
	}
	return states, nil
}

// PruneStale deletes session files older than maxAge and returns the count.
// A zero or negative maxAge disables pruning (returns 0, nil).
func (r *SessionRepository) PruneStale(maxAge time.Duration) (int, error) {
	if maxAge <= 0 {
		return 0, nil
	}
	states, err := r.ListAll()
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	pruned := 0
	for _, s := range states {
		if s.IsStale(maxAge) {
			_ = r.Delete(s.SessionID)
			pruned++
		}
	}
	return pruned, nil
}

func (r *SessionRepository) statePath(sessionID string) string {
	return filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, sessionID+".json")

		t.Errorf("expected LastEventType=%q for normal user message, got %q", "user", m.LastEventType)
	}
}

// --- Agent subagent tool name tracking tests (issue #88) ---

func TestLastOpenToolNames_AgentToolsPreservedAfterPartialResults(t *testing.T) {
	// Simulate Claude Code format: 3 streaming assistant events each with one
	// Agent tool_use, followed by 1 user event carrying a tool_result.
	// After the first result, 2 Agent calls remain open — LastOpenToolNames
	// must still contain them so InferSubagents can count them.
	path := writeTranscriptLines(t, []map[string]interface{}{
		// 3 streaming assistant chunks, each with one Agent tool_use
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(0), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": nil,
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(1), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": nil,
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		{"type": "assistant", "timestamp": ts(2), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "assistant", "stop_reason": "tool_use",
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_use", "name": "Agent"},
			},
		}},
		// First tool_result arrives (user event with embedded tool_result)
		{"type": "user", "timestamp": ts(3), "message": map[string]interface{}{
			"role": "user",
			"content": []interface{}{
				map[string]interface{}{"type": "tool_result", "content": "done"},
			},
		}},
	})

	tailer := newTestTailer(path)
	m, err := tailer.TailAndProcess()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	// 3 uses - 1 result = 2 open
	if !m.HasOpenToolCall {
		t.Error("expected HasOpenToolCall=true with 2 unmatched Agent calls")
	}
	if m.OpenToolCallCount != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected OpenToolCallCount=2, got %d", m.OpenToolCallCount)
	}

	// BUG (issue #88): ClearToolNames on the user event wipes LastOpenToolNames
	// even though tool_result blocks are present. The remaining 2 Agent names
	// should be preserved so InferSubagents can detect them.
	if len(m.LastOpenToolNames) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("expected LastOpenToolNames to have 2 entries, got %d: %v",
			len(m.LastOpenToolNames), m.LastOpenToolNames)
	}
	for i, name := range m.LastOpenToolNames {
		if name != "Agent" {
			t.Errorf("LastOpenToolNames[%d] = %q, want \"Agent\"", i, name)
		}
	}
}

{"type":"message","id":"2b630846","parentId":"337be807","timestamp":"2026-04-06T17:22:30.620Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_swlVJoAcXApmTwY5TmCVNjns|fc_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3ebd5ff8881919f0b1118373fefaf","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"package filesystem

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"irrlicht/core/domain/session"
)

const appSupportDir = "Library/Application Support/Irrlicht"

// SessionRepository implements ports/outbound.SessionRepository using the local filesystem.
type SessionRepository struct {
	instancesDir string
}

// New returns a SessionRepository rooted at the user's Application Support directory.
func New() (*SessionRepository, error) {
	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
	}
	return &SessionRepository{
		instancesDir: filepath.Join(homeDir, appSupportDir, "instances"),
	}, nil
}

// NewWithDir returns a SessionRepository rooted at the given directory (useful for tests).
func NewWithDir(dir string) *SessionRepository {
	return &SessionRepository{instancesDir: dir}
}

// InstancesDir returns the directory where session files are stored.
func (r *SessionRepository) InstancesDir() string {
	return r.instancesDir
}

// Load reads a session state from disk. Returns (nil, err) if the file does not exist.
func (r *SessionRepository) Load(sessionID string) (*session.SessionState, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(r.statePath(sessionID))
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	var state session.SessionState
	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &state, nil
}

// Save atomically writes a session state to disk.
func (r *SessionRepository) Save(state *session.SessionState) error {
	if err := os.MkdirAll(r.instancesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create instances directory: %w", err)
	}
	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(state, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal session state: %w", err)
	}
	path := r.statePath(state.SessionID)
	tmpPath := fmt.Sprintf("%s.tmp.%d.%d", path, os.Getpid(), time.Now().UnixNano())
	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp file: %w", err)
	}
	if err := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); err != nil {
		os.Remove(tmpPath)
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename temp file: %w", err)
	}
	return nil
}

// Delete removes a session state file. Returns nil if the file does not exist.
func (r *SessionRepository) Delete(sessionID string) error {
	err := os.Remove(r.statePath(sessionID))
	if err != nil && os.IsNotExist(err) {
		return nil
	}
	return err
}

// ListAll returns all session states found in the instances directory.
// Files that cannot be parsed are silently skipped.
func (r *SessionRepository) ListAll() ([]*session.SessionState, error) {
	entries, err := os.ReadDir(r.instancesDir)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return nil, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}
	var states []*session.SessionState
	for _, entry := range entries {
		if entry.IsDir() {
			continue
		}
		name := entry.Name()
		if filepath.Ext(name) != ".json" || strings.Contains(name, ".tmp.") {
			continue
		}
		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, name))
		if err != nil {
			continue
		}
		var state session.SessionState
		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
			continue
		}
		validStates := map[string]bool{
			session.StateWorking: true,
			session.StateWaiting: true,
			session.StateReady:   true,
		}
		if !validStates[state.State] {
			os.Remove(filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, name))
			continue
		}
		states = append(states, &state)
	}
	return states, nil
}

// PruneStale deletes session files older than maxAge and returns the count.
// A zero or negative maxAge disables pruning (returns 0, nil).
func (r *SessionRepository) PruneStale(maxAge time.Duration) (int, error) {
	if maxAge <= 0 {
		return 0, nil
	}
	states, err := r.ListAll()
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	pruned := 0
	for _, s := range states {
		if s.IsStale(maxAge) {
			_ = r.Delete(s.SessionID)
			pruned++
		}
	}
	return pruned, nil
}

func (r *SessionRepository) statePath(sessionID string) string {
	return filepath.Join(r.instancesDir, sessionID+".json")
}

{"type":"message","id":"28739b5c","parentId":"4682c97b","timestamp":"2026-04-06T17:22:52.161Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_LpgRE5Z2JjgIxm2Vn7LgxMqD|fc_086cf3f250712bdc0169d3ebeba494819183354b7ca5ddac48","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"package filesystem

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"irrlicht/core/domain/session"
)

// CachedSessionRepository wraps a SessionRepository with a short-lived cache
// for ListAll() to avoid redundant filesystem scans from concurrent callers.
type CachedSessionRepository struct {
	inner    *SessionRepository
	mu       sync.Mutex
	cache    []*session.SessionState
	cachedAt time.Time
	ttl      time.Duration
}

// NewCachedSessionRepository returns a caching decorator around inner with the
// given TTL for ListAll() results.
func NewCachedSessionRepository(inner *SessionRepository, ttl time.Duration) *CachedSessionRepository {
	return &CachedSessionRepository{inner: inner, ttl: ttl}
}

// Load delegates directly — single file reads are cheap.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Load(sessionID string) (*session.SessionState, error) {
	return c.inner.Load(sessionID)
}

// Save invalidates the cache then delegates to avoid stale reads.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Save(state *session.SessionState) error {
	c.invalidate()
	return c.inner.Save(state)
}

// Delete invalidates the cache then delegates to avoid stale reads.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Delete(sessionID string) error {
	c.invalidate()
	return c.inner.Delete(sessionID)
}

// ListAll returns cached results if fresh, otherwise performs the filesystem
// scan and caches the result. Returns deep copies so callers can safely mutate.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) ListAll() ([]*session.SessionState, error) {
	c.mu.Lock()
	defer c.mu.Unlock()

	if c.cache != nil && time.Since(c.cachedAt) < c.ttl {
		return deepCopySessions(c.cache), nil
	}

	states, err := c.inner.ListAll()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	c.cache = states
	c.cachedAt = time.Now()
	return deepCopySessions(states), nil
}

// InstancesDir exposes the underlying directory path.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) InstancesDir() string {
	return c.inner.InstancesDir()
}

// PruneStale delegates to inner and invalidates cache.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) PruneStale(maxAge time.Duration) (int, error) {
	n, err := c.inner.PruneStale(maxAge)
	if n > 0 {
		c.invalidate()
	}
	return n, err
}

func (c *CachedSessionRepository) invalidate() {
	c.mu.Lock()
	c.cache = nil
	c.cachedAt = time.Time{}
	c.mu.Unlock()
}

// deepCopySessions returns independent copies of session states via JSON
// round-trip. This is simple and correct — the overhead is negligible compared
// to the filesystem scan it replaces.
func deepCopySessions(src []*session.SessionState) []*session.SessionState {
	if src == nil {
		return nil
	}
	result := make([]*session.SessionState, len(src))
	for i, s := range src {
		data, _ := json.Marshal(s)
		var cp session.SessionState
		_ = json.Unmarshal(data, &cp)
		result[i] = &cp
	}
	return result
}

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"irrlicht/core/domain/session"
)

// CachedSessionRepository wraps a SessionRepository with a short-lived cache
// for ListAll() to avoid redundant filesystem scans from concurrent callers.
type CachedSessionRepository struct {
	inner    *SessionRepository
	mu       sync.Mutex
	cache    []*session.SessionState
	cachedAt time.Time
	ttl      time.Duration
}

// NewCachedSessionRepository returns a caching decorator around inner with the
// given TTL for ListAll() results.
func NewCachedSessionRepository(inner *SessionRepository, ttl time.Duration) *CachedSessionRepository {
	return &CachedSessionRepository{inner: inner, ttl: ttl}
}

// Load delegates directly — single file reads are cheap.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Load(sessionID string) (*session.SessionState, error) {
	return c.inner.Load(sessionID)
}

// Save invalidates the cache then delegates to avoid stale reads.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Save(state *session.SessionState) error {
	c.invalidate()
	return c.inner.Save(state)
}

// Delete invalidates the cache then delegates to avoid stale reads.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) Delete(sessionID string) error {
	c.invalidate()
	return c.inner.Delete(sessionID)
}

// ListAll returns cached results if fresh, otherwise performs the filesystem
// scan and caches the result. Returns deep copies so callers can safely mutate.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) ListAll() ([]*session.SessionState, error) {
	c.mu.Lock()
	defer c.mu.Unlock()

	if c.cache != nil && time.Since(c.cachedAt) < c.ttl {
		return deepCopySessions(c.cache), nil
	}

	states, err := c.inner.ListAll()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	c.cache = states
	c.cachedAt = time.Now()
	return deepCopySessions(states), nil
}

// InstancesDir exposes the underlying directory path.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) InstancesDir() string {
	return c.inner.InstancesDir()
}

// PruneStale delegates to inner and invalidates cache.
func (c *CachedSessionRepository) PruneStale(maxAge time.Duration) (int, error) {
	n, err := c.inner.PruneStale(maxAge)
	if n > 0 {
		c.invalidate()
	}
	return n, err
}

func (c *CachedSessionRepository) invalidate() {
	c.mu.Lock()
	c.cache = nil
	c.cachedAt = time.Time{}
	c.mu.Unlock()
}

// deepCopySessions returns independent copies of session states via JSON
// round-trip. This is simple and correct — the overhead is negligible compared
// to the filesystem scan it replaces.
func deepCopySessions(src []*session.SessionState) []*session.SessionState {
	if src == nil {
		return nil
	}
	result := make([]*session.SessionState, len(src))
	for i, s := range src {
		data, _ := json.Marshal(s)
		var cp session.SessionState
		_ = json.Unmarshal(data, &cp)
		result[i] = &cp
	}
	return result
}

cat > /tmp/replay_pi_state.go <<'EOF'
package main

import (
  "bufio"
  "encoding/json"
  "fmt"
  "os"
  "path/filepath"

  piadapter "irrlicht/core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi"
  "irrlicht/core/application/services"
  "irrlicht/core/domain/session"
  "irrlicht/core/pkg/tailer"
)

func main() {
  src := "/Users/ingo/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl"
  f, _ := os.Open(src)
  defer f.Close()

  tmp := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), "replay_pi_segment.jsonl")
  _ = os.Remove(tmp)
  out, _ := os.Create(tmp)
  out.Close()

  tl := tailer.NewTranscriptTailer(tmp, &piadapter.Parser{}, "pi")
  state := session.StateReady

  scanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)
  ln := 0
  for scanner.Scan() {
    ln++
    if ln < 220 || ln > 253 {
      continue
    }
    line := scanner.Text()
    af, _ := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    af.WriteString(line + "\n")
    af.Close()

    // parse metadata for log
    var raw map[string]interface{}
    _ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &raw)
    typ, _ := raw["type"].(string)
    role, stop := "", ""
    if m, ok := raw["message"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
      role, _ = m["role"].(string)
      stop, _ = m["stopReason"].(string)
    }

    m, err := tl.TailAndProcess()
    if err != nil {
      fmt.Printf("L%d ERR %v\n", ln, err)
      continue
    }
    metrics := &session.SessionMetrics{
      HasOpenToolCall: m.HasOpenToolCall,
      OpenToolCallCount: m.OpenToolCallCount,
      LastEventType: m.LastEventType,
      LastOpenToolNames: m.LastOpenToolNames,
      LastToolResultWasError: m.LastToolResultWasError,
      LastAssistantText: m.LastAssistantText,
      PermissionMode: m.PermissionMode,
    }

    if state == session.StateReady && metrics.LastEventType != "" {
      state = session.StateWorking
    }
    ns, reason := services.ClassifyState(state, metrics)
    state = ns
    fmt.Printf("L%d typ=%s role=%s stop=%s -> state=%s last=%s open=%v/%d reason=%s\n", ln, typ, role, stop, state, metrics.LastEventType, metrics.HasOpenToolCall, metrics.OpenToolCallCount, reason)
  }
}
EOF

Found the root cause in that session’s latest task.

### What’s wrong

In transcript  
`~/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl`

the latest task contains **very large single JSONL lines** (tool results from `read`):

- line **290**: ~**100,016** bytes  
- line **292**: ~**103,778** bytes  
- line **294**: ~**90,486** bytes  

### Why that breaks state detection

`core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go` does two things that together cause bad state tracking:

1. Reads only last **64KB** (`maxTailSize = 64 * 1024`)
2. Uses `bufio.Scanner` default token behavior

When a single appended line is >64KB, the tailer starts in the middle of that line (`fileSize - 64KB`), so the first “line” is partial invalid JSON and is skipped.  
That means real events (especially `toolResult`) are dropped.

### Effect on your session state

Dropped events desync open-tool accounting and last-event inference, which drives `working/ready` classification.  
You can already see inconsistent metrics in this session (e.g. open call count vs open tool names mismatch), and that causes the UI to show `ready`/`working` flips while you’re still processing.

---

So this is **not** a Pi model behavior issue; it’s a **tailer ingestion bug with oversized JSONL lines** in the latest task.  
If you want, I’ll patch the tailer next (robust line reader + oversized-line handling + tests).
patch in the pi adapter parser

package main

import (
  "bufio"
  "encoding/json"
  "fmt"
  "os"
  "path/filepath"

  piadapter "irrlicht/core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi"
  "irrlicht/core/application/services"
  "irrlicht/core/domain/session"
  "irrlicht/core/pkg/tailer"
)

type M = session.SessionMetrics

func main() {
  src := "/Users/ingo/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht--/2026-04-06T16-22-10-841Z_25f1e2bb-a884-4d5a-aca9-ec01a07929f0.jsonl"
  in, _ := os.Open(src)
  defer in.Close()

  tmp := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), "replay_pi_realbehavior.jsonl")
  _ = os.Remove(tmp)
  out, _ := os.Create(tmp)
  out.Close()

  tl := tailer.NewTranscriptTailer(tmp, &piadapter.Parser{}, "pi")
  state := session.StateReady
  var stale *M
  prevState := state

  scanner := bufio.NewScanner(in)
  scanner.Buffer(make([]byte,0,1024*1024), 2*1024*1024)
  ln := 0
  for scanner.Scan() {
    ln++
    line := scanner.Text()
    af, _ := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    af.WriteString(line+"\n")
    af.Close()

    var raw map[string]interface{}
    _ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &raw)
    typ,_ := raw["type"].(string)
    role, stop := "", ""
    if m,ok := raw["message"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
      role,_ = m["role"].(string)
      stop,_ = m["stopReason"].(string)
    }

    tm, err := tl.TailAndProcess()
    metrics := stale
    if err == nil && tm != nil {
      metrics = &M{HasOpenToolCall: tm.HasOpenToolCall, OpenToolCallCount: tm.OpenToolCallCount, LastEventType: tm.LastEventType, LastOpenToolNames: tm.LastOpenToolNames, LastToolResultWasError: tm.LastToolResultWasError, LastAssistantText: tm.LastAssistantText, PermissionMode: tm.PermissionMode}
      stale = metrics
    }

    if state == session.StateReady && metrics != nil && metrics.LastEventType != "" {
      state = session.StateWorking
    }
    ns, reason := services.ClassifyState(state, metrics)
    state = ns

    if state != prevState || ln>=286 {
      var le string
      var open bool
      var oc int
      if metrics!=nil { le=metrics.LastEventType; open=metrics.HasOpenToolCall; oc=metrics.OpenToolCallCount }
      fmt.Printf("L%d typ=%s role=%s stop=%s err=%v => %s (last=%s open=%v/%d) %s\n", ln, typ, role, stop, err!=nil, state, le, open, oc, reason)
      prevState = state
    }
  }
}
EOF

arguments.command:
cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht && gh pr edit 91 --add-label bug --body-file - <<'EOF'
## Summary
- treat Pi `type: "compaction"` transcript entries as activity events instead of skipping them
- map compaction to `assistant` activity so ready sessions transition to `working`
- pull stale-tool waiting behavior behind adapter policies
  - add `agent.StatePolicy`
  - add `StatePolicy()` in Claude Code, Codex, and Pi adapters
  - wire policies into `SessionDetector` via `SetAdapterPolicies(...)`
  - disable stale-tool timeout waiting for Pi to avoid false `waiting` while long-running tools are still processing
- fix tailer ingestion for large JSONL appends (>64KB)
  - continue from `lastOffset` once established (no jumping into mid-line)
  - increase scanner token buffer to handle large tool results
  - align initial truncated reads to newline boundary
- add regression test: large appended tool_result line is parsed and closes open calls

## Verification
- `cd core && go test ./pkg/tailer -count=1`
- `cd core && go test ./... -count=1`
EOF

content.text: https://github.com/ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht/pull/91

Continued and applied the fix.

I found this could not be fixed purely in `pi/parser.go` because the events were being lost **before** parser invocation (tailer started mid-line when >64KB was appended). I patched the ingestion path accordingly.

### ✅ Changes made

- **`core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go`**
  - Use `lastOffset` as authoritative incremental cursor once set (no skip-ahead into new large lines).
  - Increased scanner max token size to handle large JSONL entries.
  - Align initial truncated read to next newline boundary to avoid parsing partial JSON lines.

- **`core/pkg/tailer/tool_call_test.go`**
  - Added regression test:
    - `TestTailAndProcess_LargeAppendedToolResult_NotSkipped`
    - Verifies a >64KB appended `tool_result` is parsed and closes open tool calls.

### ✅ Validation

- `cd core && go test ./pkg/tailer -count=1` passed
- `cd core && go test ./... -count=1` passed

### ✅ Commit / Push

- Commit: `b3e6c1a`
- Branch: `fix/pi-compaction-working-state`
- Pushed to origin and PR updated:
  - https://github.com/ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht/pull/91

### ✅ Local deploy

I rebuilt and restarted local dev stack (`/tmp/irrlichd` + debug macOS app), so you can test immediately.

If you want, I can now tail live state transitions for that session ID while you run another Pi task and confirm no ready/working oscillation.
